sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The agent moved RIGHT. Looking at the image, the agent (blue square) is now at the top right corner. There's also a gray square at the left side of the top corridor, which might be a "checkpoint" or "starting position" marker.
Let me check the agent's current position and try moving DOWN to follow the red path.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check agent position
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] > 5:
        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check for gray objects
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'g':
        print(f"Gray: id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check maze around agent
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nRows 7-15, cols 33-43:")
for i in range(7, 16):
    row = ""
    for j in range(33, 44):
        ch = ascii_lines[i][j]
        if ch == 'W':
            row += '.'
        elif ch == 'B':
            row += '#'
        elif ch == 'R':
            row += 'R'
        elif ch == 'b':
            row += '@'
        elif ch == 'g':
            row += 'G'
        else:
            row += '?'
    print(f"Row {i:2d}: {row}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The agent is at rows 8-12, cols 38-42. The agent moved RIGHT from cols 14-18 to cols 38-42. The gray object at rows 20-24, cols 14-18 is a "checkpoint" or "starting position" marker that appeared when the agent moved away from that area.
Looking at the ascii at rows 7-15, cols 33-43:
Row 7: ###########
Row 8: #####@@@@@#
Row 9: #####@@@@@#
Row 10: #####@@#@@#
Row 11: #####@@@@@#
Row 12: #####@@@@@#
Row 13: #######R###
Row 14: ......#R#..
Row 15: ......#R#..
The agent is at rows 8-12, cols 38-42. Below the agent at rows 13-15, cols 38-42:
Row 13: #######R### (cols 33-39 is #######, col 40 is R, cols 41-43 is ###)
Row 14: ......#R#.. (cols 33-38 is ......, cols 39-42 is #R#., col 43 is .)
Row 15: ......#R#.. (same)
So rows 13-15, cols 39-41 has #R# (black wall with red path). The agent can move DOWN to rows 13-17, cols 38-42.
But wait, row 13, cols 33-39 is #######, which means cols 33-39 is black wall. The agent is at cols 38-42, so the agent can move DOWN if rows 13-17, cols 38-42 is within the black area.
Row 13: #######R### (cols 33-39 is #######, col 40 is R, cols 41-43 is ###)
Row 14: ......#R#.. (cols 33-38 is ......, cols 39-42 is #R#.)
So row 13, cols 38-42 is #R### (black wall with red path). Row 14, cols 38-42 is .#R#. (white passage with black wall and red path).
The agent can't move DOWN because row 14, cols 38-42 has white passages.
But wait, the red path is at cols 39-41, rows 13-40. The agent can move DOWN along the red path.
Let me try moving DOWN to see if the agent can move through the red path area.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move DOWN to follow the red path
action(['DOWN', 'DOWN', 'DOWN', 'DOWN', 'DOWN'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
